name: One-Skillet Spaghetti
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook and stir meat and onions in a large skillet or Dutch oven until the meat is brown.
2. Drain off any excess fat.
3. Stir in tomatoes (with their liquid) and the remaining ingredients, except the Cheddar cheese. Break up the tomatoes.
4. To cook in the skillet: Heat the mixture to boiling. Reduce heat; cover and simmer, stirring occasionally, until the spaghetti is tender, about 30 minutes. If necessary, add a small amount of water. Sprinkle with cheese. Cover and heat until the cheese is melted.
5. To cook in the oven: Pour the mixture into an ungreased 2- or 2 1/2-quart casserole. Cover and bake at 375°F for about 45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the spaghetti is tender. Uncover; sprinkle with shredded Cheddar cheese and bake about 5 minutes more.
